# macOS LaunchAgents — Privilege Escalation via User-Space Persistence

**Platform:** macos · **Category:** persistence · **Severity:** medium

Writable LaunchAgent/LaunchDaemon directories let an attacker register a payload that macOS launches automatically at login or boot.

**MITRE ATT&CK:** [T1543.001 — Create or Modify System Process: Launch Agent](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1543/001/)
## Description

macOS starts background tasks via `LaunchAgents` (`.plist` files), loaded from `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` for the current user or `/Library/LaunchAgents/` system-wide. `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` alone does not cross a privilege boundary — it runs as the current user — but it becomes a genuine escalation vector once an attacker can write to a system-level LaunchAgents/LaunchDaemons directory, or where a privileged LaunchDaemon can be induced to load a user-controlled binary. Even limited to user space, it is a reliable stealthy persistence and lateral-movement primitive.

## Requirements

- Write access to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ (default for user accounts)
- A script or binary to execute
- Optional, for real privilege escalation rather than persistence: write access to /Library/LaunchAgents/ or /Library/LaunchDaemons/
## Exploitation

1. Craft a .plist file whose ProgramArguments point at a payload.
2. Place it in ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ (or a system LaunchAgents/LaunchDaemons directory if writable).
3. Load it immediately with `launchctl load <path>`, or wait for the next login/boot.
4. The system executes the payload as the owning context (current user, or root for a system LaunchDaemon).
## Proof of Concept

- [`poc.sh`](./poc.sh)
- [`poc_reverse_shell.sh`](./poc_reverse_shell.sh)
## Detection

| Type | Artifact | Description |
|------|----------|--------------|
| sigma | [`detections/sigma/macos_launch_agents.yml`](./detections/sigma/macos_launch_agents.yml) | Flags creation of .plist files under LaunchAgents directories. |
| osquery | [`detections/osquery/macos_launch_agents.conf`](./detections/osquery/macos_launch_agents.conf) | Periodically enumerates user-level LaunchAgent plists. |

## Mitigation

- Enforce least privilege on ~/Library/LaunchAgents/, /Library/LaunchAgents/, and /Library/LaunchDaemons/.
- Enable SIP and require code signing for loaded agents/daemons where possible.
- File-integrity-monitor LaunchAgent/LaunchDaemon directories and alert on new or modified plists.
- Audit `launchctl load`/`launchctl bootstrap` invocations.
## References

- [MITRE ATT&CK T1543.001](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1543/001/)
- [Objective-See: Malware Persistence on macOS](https://objective-see.org/blog/blog_0x26.html)
